parameters.jsCode:
const policyText = `
TETHERFI CUSTOMER SUPPORT POLICY DOCUMENT

SECTION 1: WIFI ISSUES

Issue: Customer cannot connect to WiFi
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer to restart their router by unplugging for 30 seconds and plugging back in.
2. Check if the WiFi light on the router is stable green. If blinking red, there is a line issue.
3. Ask customer to forget the network on their device and reconnect with the correct password.
4. If issue persists after restart, escalate to technical team within 2 hours.
5. Offer a callback within 4 hours if remote fix is not possible.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 1 hour. Resolve within 24 hours.

Issue: WiFi speed is slow
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer to run a speed test at www.speedtest.net and share results.
2. If speed is below 50% of subscribed plan, escalate to network team immediately.
3. Suggest customer place router in a central location away from walls.
4. Offer a free technician visit if remote steps do not resolve the issue.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 1 hour. Resolve within 48 hours.

Issue: WiFi keeps disconnecting
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer to check if disconnection happens on all devices or just one.
2. If all devices, check for network outage in the area.
3. Reset router to factory settings if no outage is found.
4. Schedule technician visit if problem continues after reset.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 1 hour. Resolve within 24 hours.

SECTION 2: BROADBAND ISSUES

Issue: No internet connection
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer to check if all cable connections to the router are secure.
2. Check the broadband light on the router. If red or off, there is a line fault.
3. Run a remote line diagnostic from the support portal.
4. If line fault is confirmed, raise a field engineer ticket immediately.
5. Field engineer will visit within 24 hours on working days.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 30 minutes. Resolve within 24 hours.

Issue: Broadband speed much lower than plan
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer to connect device directly to router via ethernet cable and test speed.
2. If wired speed is also low, the issue is with the line not the WiFi.
3. Run a remote speed and line quality test from support portal.
4. If line quality is degraded, escalate to network team for line investigation.
5. Offer temporary data compensation if issue takes more than 48 hours to resolve.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 1 hour. Resolve within 48 hours.

SECTION 3: BILLING AND PAYMENT ISSUES

Issue: Customer charged incorrectly
Resolution Steps:
1. Pull up the customer billing history from the CRM system.
2. If overcharge is confirmed, raise a refund request immediately.
3. Refund will be processed within 5 to 7 working days to the original payment method.
4. Send a written confirmation of the refund to the customer registered email.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 2 hours. Resolve within 7 working days.

Issue: Payment not reflecting in account
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer for payment reference number or transaction ID.
2. Check payment gateway records for the transaction.
3. If payment is found but not updated, manually reconcile the account.
4. Resolve within 24 hours. If delayed beyond 48 hours notify the customer proactively.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 1 hour. Resolve within 24 hours.

SECTION 4: ROUTER AND EQUIPMENT ISSUES

Issue: Router not powering on
Resolution Steps:
1. Ask customer to check if the power adapter is properly connected.
2. If router still does not power on, arrange a replacement router within 2 working days.
3. Provide prepaid return label for the faulty router.
Response Time: Acknowledge within 1 hour. Resolve within 2 working days.

SECTION 5: ESCALATION POLICY

1. If any issue cannot be resolved within the first 2 email exchanges, escalate to senior support team immediately.
2. For high priority customers or business accounts, response time is reduced to 30 minutes.
3. Any legal complaints must be escalated to the legal team within 1 hour.
4. If a customer mentions they will switch providers, flag as churn risk and escalate to retention team.

SECTION 6: TONE AND COMMUNICATION GUIDELINES

1. Always greet the customer by their first name.
2. Use a professional, friendly, and empathetic tone in all communications.
3. Always acknowledge the customer frustration before jumping to solutions.
4. Always end every email with: Best regards, Tetherfi Support Team.
5. Do not share internal system names, pricing structures, or employee details with customers.

SECTION 7: REFUND POLICY

1. Refunds are applicable within 30 days of the charge date.
2. Customer must provide proof of payment such as transaction ID or screenshot.
3. Refunds are processed within 5 to 7 working days.
4. Refunds are credited back to the original payment method only.
5. Service credits can be offered as an alternative to cash refunds.
`;

const chunkSize = 400;
const overlap = 50;
const chunks = [];

for (let i = 0; i < policyText.length; i += chunkSize - overlap) {
  const chunk = policyText.slice(i, i + chunkSize);
  if (chunk.trim().length > 0) {
    chunks.push({
      json: {
        pageContent: chunk,
        metadata: { source: 'tetherfi_support_policy' }
      }
    });
  }
}

return chunks;

parameters.name: policy_retriever
parameters.description: Retrieves relevant Tetherfi customer support policies including WiFi issues, broadband issues, billing issues, router problems, escalation rules and tone guidelines. Always call this before drafting any reply.

categories.description: Customer has WiFi connectivity problems, slow speed, or keeps disconnecting
Customer has no internet, broadband dropping, or slow broadband speed
Customer has payment problems, wrong charges, refund requests, or bill disputes
Customer has router hardware problems, lights showing error, or router not working
Urgent issues, customer threatening to leave, legal complaints, or business account issues

parameters.text:
=You are a professional customer support email assistant for Tetherfi — a telecom and internet service provider.

You have access to Tetherfi's customer support policies via the policy_retriever tool.
ALWAYS call policy_retriever FIRST before drafting any reply.

Email details:
From: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.from }}
Subject: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.subject }}
Message: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.snippet }}
Category: {{ $('Text Classifier').item.json.category }}

Instructions:
1. Call policy_retriever with the issue type to get relevant resolution steps
2. Draft a professional reply based strictly on those policies
3. Include the exact resolution steps from the policy
4. Keep tone professional, friendly, and empathetic
5. Acknowledge the customer's frustration first
6. Address the customer by name if available in the email
7. End with: Best regards, Tetherfi Support Team

Draft the reply now.

=You are a tone correction expert for Tetherfi customer support emails.

Review the draft email below and correct the tone if needed.
Rules:
- Must be professional and friendly
- Must NOT be casual or use slang
- Must be empathetic to the customer
- Must be clear and concise
- Must include all resolution steps from the draft
- Must end with: Best regards, Tetherfi Support Team
- Do NOT change the actual information, policy content or resolution steps

Draft to review:
{{ $('AI Agent (Draft Reply)').item.json.output }}

Return ONLY the final corrected email. No explanations.
